excluded_reason: Consumer-side module tables (chartsearchai chat/embedding/audit + querystore bootstrap marker). They are recreated empty when the consuming module installs against the demo; chartsearchai_chat_message also carried harness test-chat history that is not demo data.
source: Transform of OpenMRS large-demo-data-2-7-0.sql via feature 002 SQLMesh + dlt pipeline. This build adds the deterministic preferred-address normalization (see remediation_evidence.preferred_address) so the O3 patient chart renders addresses. Supersedes the 2026-06-03 and 2026-05-19 publishes; clinical row counts are unchanged.

pipeline_provenance.demo_regen_commit_subject: Demo-data regen: referentially clean, fully preserved, promotable (#20)
pipeline_provenance.preferred_address_commit_subject: feat(002): derive preferred address in stg_person_address so O3 renders it

remediation_evidence._note: Every value below was re-derived from the live promoted openmrs schema during this dump, not copied forward from a prior publish.

drug_catalog_coverage.synthetic_drug_id_rule: drug_id = 300000 + CIEL concept numeric (e.g. 300633 -> CIEL 633 Efavirenz)

preferred_address._note: The legacy 2.7 corpus marks no preferred address (all 0), so OpenMRS FHIR emits every address with use=old and O3 renders no address. The transform now deterministically flags one non-voided address per person preferred. This is a demo-display normalization, NOT source-faithful for the preferred column.

known_patient.name: Zabella Talai Halambe
known_patient.evidence_note: This patient's drug-order concepts resolve through CIEL UUIDs to correctly-named antiretroviral concepts, confirming the concept-FK-through-CIEL remediation.

verified_load.target: clean mariadb:10.11.7 container (MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD=test, utf8mb4 server charset)
